name: Mittelalter
prompt: Transform this family tree into a medieval manuscript illustration. The background should resemble aged parchment with warm brown tones, decorated with simple knotwork borders in red and gold. Connecting lines should be subtle vine tendrils with small leaves. Decorative elements include small heraldic shields and simple floral motifs in the margins. The palette uses earthy tones: ochre, red, and gold, with a natural, hand-drawn appearance and visible parchment texture

name: Barock
prompt: A 17th-century copperplate engraving on laid paper, featuring visible foxing and aged patina. The artwork uses fine cross-hatching for shading, with text rendered in Roman Square Capitals for primary titles and Chancery-style Italics. The color palette is strictly monochrome, dominated by carbon ink with subtle oxidized sepia tones. The composition is symmetrical, centered around an arboreal (tree-like) structure, with directional lighting emanating from the top-left corner. The header text reads 'Fol. 11.' in the same historical style.

name: Art Nouveau
prompt: Transform this family tree into a stunning Art Nouveau decorative poster in the style of Alphonse Mucha, circa 1900. The background features flowing sinuous curves and organic natural forms — lily pads, irises, wisteria blossoms, and peacock feathers. Connecting lines become elegant curved stems and intertwining botanical vines. The border is a graceful arched frame of stylized flowers and flowing feminine hair. The color palette is soft pastel — sage green, dusty rose, gold, ivory, and lavender — with delicate watercolor gradients and precise fine-line details. Ornamental circles and halos frame the composition. Printed poster aesthetic with visible grain texture

name: Realistischer Baum
prompt: Transform this family tree into a photorealistic painting of a magnificent ancient oak tree. The tree trunk rises from fertile earth at the bottom of the image, with large gnarled roots spreading wide. Each generation of the family is represented by a sturdy branch growing outward, with smaller twigs for individuals. The rectangular boxes are integrated as natural wooden plaques or stone tablets. Warm afternoon sunlight filters through a lush green canopy. Background shows a rolling pastoral landscape with meadows and a river. Painted in the style of a 19th-century Romantic landscape — rich earthy tones, dappled light, and atmospheric depth

name: Antik / Griechisch-Römisch
prompt: Transform this family tree into a classical Greco-Roman stone inscription relief. The background resembles aged white marble with subtle grey veining and carved texture. Connecting lines become carved stone borders and classical meander (Greek key) patterns etched in relief. The borders are adorned with carved laurel wreaths, Roman eagles, ionic column capitals, and amphora motifs. Decorative elements include Greek pottery-style red and black figure illustrations in the margins. The overall aesthetic is a monumental marble stele or triumphal arch inscription panel. Slight weathering and patina on the stone surface. Color palette: marble white, travertine cream, terracotta red, and verdigris bronze

name: Minimalistisch
prompt: Transform this family tree into a clean, minimalist design. The background is plain white or light grey. Connecting lines are thin, straight, and unobtrusive, with subtle rounded corners. The boxes are simple rectangles with a thin border and no shadows. The color palette is monochromatic: black text on white or light grey background, with a single accent color (e.g., soft blue or green) for highlights. The layout is spacious, with ample white space and a modern, uncluttered aesthetic

name: Japanisch / Ukiyo-e
prompt: Transform this family tree into a traditional Japanese woodblock print (Ukiyo-e). The background features a textured paper with subtle woodgrain. Connecting lines are delicate, flowing lines reminiscent of calligraphic brushstrokes. The boxes are designed as floating scrolls or lanterns, with Japanese characters for names. Decorative elements include cherry blossoms, waves, and cranes. The color palette is traditional: indigo, crimson, gold, and black, with a hand-printed aesthetic

name: Futuristisch
prompt: Transform this family tree into a futuristic holographic display. The background is a dark, gradient mesh with a subtle grid or particle effect. Connecting lines are glowing neon tubes or light trails, with nodes as floating holographic panels. The boxes are translucent glass-like panels with digital text. Decorative elements include geometric patterns, data streams, and glowing orbs. The color palette is vibrant neon: electric blue, magenta, cyan, and white, with a high-tech, sci-fi aesthetic

name: Skizze / Bleistift
prompt: Transform this family tree into a hand-drawn pencil sketch. The background is plain white or lightly textured paper. Connecting lines are rough, sketchy pencil lines with visible strokes. The boxes are simple, hand-drawn rectangles with shaded edges. Decorative elements include cross-hatching, smudges, and eraser marks. The color palette is monochromatic: various shades of grey and black, with a raw, unfinished look

name: Renaissance
prompt: Transform this family tree into a Renaissance-style fresco or illuminated manuscript. The background resembles aged plaster or vellum with a warm, creamy tone. Connecting lines are elegant, flowing ribbons or gold leaf filigree. The boxes are designed as ornate cartouches or architectural frames, with Latin-inspired typography. Decorative elements include cherubs, grotesque masks, acanthus scrolls, and classical columns. The color palette is rich and balanced: deep blues, terracotta, gold, and olive green, evoking the works of Botticelli or Michelangelo. Subtle cracking and aging effects add authenticity

name: 19th Century Romanticism
prompt: Transform this family tree into a Romantic-era painting, inspired by the works of Caspar David Friedrich or J.M.W. Turner. The background is a dramatic, atmospheric landscape with misty mountains, stormy skies, or a serene forest. Connecting lines are organic, almost like winding rivers or tree branches. The boxes are integrated as stone tablets, ancient ruins, or floating parchment scrolls. The color palette is moody and expressive: deep greens, stormy grays, warm browns, and golden highlights. The overall mood is emotional, mysterious, and sublime, with a focus on nature and the sublime
